title: Petrit Halilaj: An Opera Out of Time
description: Petrit Halilaj will present his first major institutional solo exhibition in Berlin at Hamburger Bahnhof. The focus is on the artist's first opera work, “Syrigana”

title: Saâdane Afif: Five Preludes
description: The focus of Saâdane Afif’s exhibition at Hamburger Bahnhof is his multi-piece work, The Fountain Archives, which was given to the Nationalgalerie in 2023 and is being presented to the public for the first time at Hamburger Bahnhof.

title: UNICEF Photo of the Year 2025
description: Every year, UNICEF Germany holds the international “UNICEF Photo of the Year” competition to recognize images and reports by professional photojournalists that document the personalities and living conditions of children.

title: James Nachtwey: Memoria
description: For more than four decades, James Nachtwey documented conflicts, injustices, and the fragile efforts to preserve a shred of humanity amid these crises.

title: Built community: Göbeklitepe, Taş Tepeler, and life 12,000 years ago
description: Humans became sedentary for the very first time around 12,000 years ago, at the beginning of the Holocene.

title: Graciela Iturbide: Eyes to Fly With
description: C/O Berlin presents the first major retrospective of Graciela Iturbide in Berlin, offering a deep insight into the work of one of Mexico's most important photographers.

title: Bosporus Beats: Views of Istanbul from 1500 to 1800
description: Works on paper depicting the cosmopolitan city of Istanbul and its inhabitants are the focus of the exhibition.

title: Reiner Maria Matysik: Sextinction – What basically has no name or: The reinvention of nature
description: Reiner Maria Matysik uses instruments from the natural sciences and the visual arts for his speculative biology of the future.

title: Gianna Surangkanjanajai: Upcoming
description: Gianna Surangkanjanajai works primarily in sculpture, attending to situations in which form seems to surface from the conditions that surround it.

title: Klara Lidén: Kunstwerke
description: The exhibition “Kunstwerke” (Artworks) by Berlin-based artist Klara Lidén spans three floors of KW and brings together key works from the early 2000s to the present day, including performances, sculptures, spatial interventions, and video works.

title: Stephanie Comilang – IBB Video Space
description: In her work, Stephanie Comilang engages themes of labour, technology and postcolonial entanglement in the context of global mobility.

title: Giulia Andreani: Sabotage
description: In February 2026, painter Giulia Andreani opens the exhibition programme marking the 30th anniversary of Hamburger Bahnhof, presenting paintings that expose fractures within official histories.

title: Avantgarde: Max Liebermann and Impressionism in Germany
description: With his Impressionist painting, Max Liebermann initiated the first avant-garde movement in Germany.

title: Edith Tudor-Hart: Crossing Lines – Photography between art and activism
description: Edith Tudor-Hart, née Suschitzky, was a key figure in social documentary photography between 1930 and 1955.

title: Peter Hujar und Liz Deschenes: Persistence of Vision
description: The exhibition Persistence of Vision brings together the work of Peter Hujar and Liz Deschenes, placing them in a cross-generational dialogue about photography.

title: Brancusi
description: In cooperation with the Centre Pompidou in Paris, the Neue Nationalgalerie presents the first major exhibition of this exceptional artist in Germany in over 50 years.

title: Seeing Words, Reading Images – The Written Art Collection in dialogue with the Deutsche Bank Collection
description: The exhibition focuses on the contemporary perspective, a kind of worldwide written art that centers on the global dialogue of today's artistic positions.

title: Iliggocene: The Age of Dizziness
description: “Iliggocene: The Age of Dizziness” at Kindl presents artists' positions that seek new vocabularies for these states of vertigo.

title: Simon Faithfull: Earth-ling – M1 VideoSpace
description: Simon Faithfull's exhibition “Earth-ling” brings together videos, photographs, and sculptures in which a figure (or body) encounters the living fabric of a planet.

title: Barbara Loftus: A Disinheritance
description: The exhibition “Eine Enterbung” is the result of an artistic exploration, ongoing since 1994, by British painter and filmmaker Barbara Loftus into the earlier life of her mother, Hildegard.

title: Shilpa Gupta: What Still Holds
description: Starting point of Shilpa Gupta’s solo exhibition at Hamburger Bahnhof is the monumental work Truth, situated at the intersection of language, power, and control.

title: Marc Brandenburg: 20th Century Debris
description: The exhibition at the Berlinische Galerie is dedicated to the multifaceted work of Marc Brandenburg, who has been shaping the Berlin art scene for decades.

title: New Woman, New Vision: Women Photographers of the Bauhaus
venue: Museum für Fotografie - Helmut Newton Stiftung
description: The female photographers at the Bauhaus experimented with photographic and design techniques, exploring the limits of their medium.

title: Ein Ticket für alle Ausstellungen
description: Marina Abramović's exhibition at Berlin's Gropis Bau focuses on her ongoing interest in rituals, eroticism, death, and the body as a site of political resistance.

title: Intermezzo: Revisiting Helmut Newton
venue: Museum für Fotografie - Helmut Newton Stiftung
description: The new permanent exhibition at the Museum of Photography will respond even more dynamically to the different aspects of Helmut and June Newton's photographic works.

title: Heavy Fabric Women – Traditional Costume – Life Stories
